content:
I18N: English | 简体中文 | 日本語
Official Channels If you have technical questions for discussion or sharing, feel free to join the following channels:
QQ Group: OpenWRT Firmware Technical Research Group, Group Number is 891659613. Join the group: Link.
Click to download QQ client. Telegram Group: OpenWRT Firmware Technical Research Group. Join the group: Link.
ArmSoM Sige Board Series Introduction ArmSoM-Sige Series: Your All-in-One Powerhouse for Soft Routing, SBCs, Mini Servers, and Home Automation.
ArmSoM Store
Buy Link: Notice Never compile OpenWRT as root If you are living in mainland China, please make sure you could visit the REAL Internet. Default login IP is 192.168.1.1, password is password. How to Compile Install a Linux distribution, Debian or Ubuntu LTS is recommended.
Install dependencies:
1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 sudo apt update -y sudo apt full-upgrade -y sudo apt install -y ack antlr3 asciidoc autoconf automake autopoint binutils bison build-essential \ bzip2 ccache clang cmake cpio curl device-tree-compiler flex gawk gcc-multilib g++-multilib gettext \ git gperf haveged help2man intltool libc6-dev-i386 libelf-dev libfuse-dev libglib2.0-dev libgmp3-dev \ libltdl-dev libmpc-dev libmpfr-dev libncurses5-dev libncursesw5-dev libpython3-dev libreadline-dev \ libssl-dev libtool llvm lrzsz mkisofs msmtp nano ninja-build p7zip p7zip-full patch pkgconf python3 \ python3-pyelftools python3-setuptools qemu-utils rsync scons squashfs-tools subversion swig texinfo \ uglifyjs upx-ucl unzip vim wget xmlto xxd zlib1g-dev Clone the source code, update feeds and configure:
1 2 3 4 5 git clone https://github.com/coolsnowwolf/lede cd lede ./scripts/feeds update -a ./scripts/feeds install -a make menuconfig Download libraries and compile firmware
(-j is the thread count, single-thread is recommended for the first build):
1 2 make download -j8 make V=s -j1 These commands are supposed to compile the source code successfully. All source code of R23 is included, including IPK.
You can use this source code freely, but please link this GitHub repository when redistributing. Thank you for your cooperation!
Rebuild:
1 2 3 4 5 6 7 cd lede git pull ./scripts/feeds update -a ./scripts/feeds install -a make defconfig make download -j8 make V=s -j$(nproc) If reconfiguration is need:
1 2 3 rm -rf ./tmp && rm -rf .config make menuconfig make V=s -j$(nproc) Build artifacts will be outputted to bin/targets directory.
If you are using WSL/WSL2 as your build environment WSL’s PATH potentially contain Windows paths with spaces, which may cause compilation failure. Please add the following lines to your local environment profiles before compiling:
1 2 3 4 5 # Update and reload your profile, ~/.bashrc for example. cat << EOF >> ~/.bashrc export PATH="/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:$PATH" EOF source ~/.bashrc NTFS-formatted drives mounted to a WSL distribution will be case-insensitive by default. This will cause the following error when compiling in WSL/WSL2:
1 Build dependency: OpenWrt can only be built on a case-sensitive filesystem A simple solution is to create a case-sensitive directory for the repository before git clone:
1 2 3 4 # Open a terminal as administrator PS > fsutil.exe file setCaseSensitiveInfo <your_local_lede_path> enable # Clone this repository to the case-sensitive directory <your_local_lede_path> PS > git clone git@github.com:coolsnowwolf/lede.git <your_local_lede_path> For directories that have already been git clone, fsutil.exe will not take effect. Case sensitivity will only be enabled for new changes in the directory.
macOS Compilation Install Xcode from AppStore
Install Homebrew:
1 /usr/bin/ruby -e "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/master/install)" Install toolchain, dependencies and packages with Homebrew:
1 2 3 brew unlink awk brew install coreutils diffutils findutils gawk gnu-getopt gnu-tar grep make ncurses pkg-config wget quilt xz brew install gcc@11 Update your system environment:
mac with intel chip 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/opt/coreutils/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/opt/findutils/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/opt/gnu-getopt/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/opt/gnu-tar/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/opt/grep/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/opt/gnu-sed/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/opt/make/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc mac with apple chip 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/coreutils/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/findutils/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/gnu-getopt/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/gnu-tar/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/grep/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/gnu-sed/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/make/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc Reload your shell profile source ~/.bashrc && bash, then you can compile normally like Linux.
Declaration This source code doesn’t contain any backdoors or closed source applications that can monitor/capture your HTTPS traffic. SSL security is the final castle of cyber security. Safety is what a firmware should do. Want to learn OpenWRT development but don’t know how to start? Can’t motivate yourself for self-learning? Do not have enough fundamental knowledge? Learn OpenWRT development with Mr. Zuo through his Beginner OpenWRT Training Course. Click here to register. QCA IPQ60xx open source repository: https://github.com/coolsnowwolf/openwrt-gl-ax1800 OpenWRT Archive repository: https://github.com/coolsnowwolf/openwrt Introduction to Software Routers Yingku R2 - N95/N300 Mini Four-Network HomeLab Server
(Introduction page - Yingku Technology (support AliPay Huabei)):
